rationale: The round-2 delta (389fcc41) is a seam-transparent boundary reroute with ZERO gate-behavior change. Both wrappers delegate byte-for-byte: lifecycle.IsAncestor(workdir, ancestor, descendant) (bool, error) and lifecycle.BranchExists(name) bool have signatures identical to gitutil's (verified: gitops.go:446 / :94), and each body is a bare `return gitutil.X(...)` — no arg reordering, no error wrapping, no default masking. The injection seams implIsAncestorFn/implBranchExistsFn keep identical names/types, so every AC test binds unchanged: the o4fd fail-closed guarantee, the four legs' fail-directions (BranchExists absent-or-probe-failure = 'no trigger', never false-refuse), the Option-B TOCTOU closure, and the hatch non-bypass all read on the same code paths. Verified empirically: spot tests (Orphan|WorktreeEnum|Obligation|Hatch) PASS; TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks PASS; `go list` confirms internal/approve no longer imports internal/gitutil (ADR-0030 honored without allowlist); build+vet clean; no import cycle (approve→lifecycle pre-existed; lifecycle→gitutil pre-existed). My round-1 security conclusion — no un-gated path beyond the 3 disclosed residuals — is untouched. No new security gap. git clean.
